Summary: This community in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty members Ajay Singh and Rohit Buddhiram Chaurasiya. They supervise PhD students Mohd Ilyas Khan, Navin Govind Singhaniya, Ninad Bandhu Kamble, Soleha Kousar, and Sweta Kumari in research areas including communication theory, cognitive radios, and physical layer security. The group's work spans various aspects of electrical engineering, with a focus on theoretical foundations and practical applications in signal processing, information theory, and VLSI design.
reports.summary: This community in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty members Ajay Singh and Rohit Buddhiram Chaurasiya. The group's work spans various aspects of electrical engineering, with a focus on theoretical foundations and practical applications in signal processing, information theory, and VLSI design.

His PhD student, Ankit Pandey, is working under his supervision on related research areas, including RF & Microwave Engineering. This group's focus is on computational electromagnetics, specifically the FDTD method, indicating a strong emphasis on numerical modeling and simulation in electromagnetic fields.

Summary: This community in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Ankit Dubey and consists of PhD students Burhan Wafai, Parwez Alam, Suchitra Tiwari, Shashi Bhushan Kotwal, Yasin Khan, and External Persons Chinmoy Kundu and Amit Kumar Singh. The group's research focuses on various areas including Communications Theory, Diversity Combining, Multihop Transmission, Machine-to-Machine Communication, and Smart Solutions/IoT, with specific projects such as Physical layer security in wireless communication and Metasurface Lens Antenna for 5G Applications. They have also mentored a startup called Data Sailors and supervised several PhD students.
reports.summary: This community in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Ankit Dubey and consists of PhD students Burhan Wafai, Parwez Alam, Suchitra Tiwari, Shashi Bhushan Kotwal, Yasin Khan, and External Persons Chinmoy Kundu and Amit Kumar Singh. Summary: The Community 7 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty member Arun Kumar Verma. His PhD students, including ABHISHEK VERMA, Akash Deo, Hemant Gupta, and Vivek Bhadwaj, are working under his supervision on research areas such as power electronics, renewable energy converters, electric vehicle chargers, and bi-directional power flow. This group's focus is on sustainable and intelligent off-grid solar PV systems, on-board EV chargers, and fast EV charging technologies, with a specific emphasis on power converter design and magnetics design for electric vehicles.

  ResearchArea: Grid connected hybrid multivelvel converter topology design, Flexible AC Transmission Systems (FACTs), High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC), Grid integration of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), Battery Management System (BMS), V2G and G2V EV charging applications, 1. Summary: Community 10 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is a research-focused group consisting of faculty members Ibhanchand Rath, Nalin Kumar Sharma, Priyanka Mishra, Priyatosh Jena, Shikha Baghel, and Soma S Dhavala. The group conducts research in various areas, including grid-connected hybrid multilevel converter topology design, Flexible AC Transmission Systems (FACTs), High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC), and the integration of renewable energy sources with Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS). They also explore applications such as Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) and Grid-to-Vehicle (G2V) electric vehicle charging. The group's research is characterized by a multidisciplinary approach, incorporating aspects of control systems, robotics, intelligent transportation systems, and reinforcement learning.
reports.summary: Community 10 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is a research-focused group consisting of faculty members Ibhanchand Rath, Nalin Kumar Sharma, Priyanka Mishra, Priyatosh Jena, Shikha Baghel, and Soma S Dhavala. They also explore applications such as Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) and Grid-to-Vehicle (G2V) electric vehicle charging. Summary: The Community 19 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is a research group focused on security and reliability of electronic circuits. They have received funding from the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), Government of India, for two projects: "Design and Investigation of Reliability Aware Security Circuits" and "Electromagnetic Side-channel Attack Analysis on FPGA based Systems". The group's work likely involves designing secure and reliable electronic systems, with a focus on preventing side-channel attacks.
